Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Olympic games latest: Day two update from Paris

Get your latest update on the progress of South African athletes on day two of the Games of the XXXIII Olympiad in Paris.

Team South Africa’s Olympic efforts are in full swing at Paris 2024 as the action continues on day two of the Olympiad.

Individual and team efforts have already proudly represented South Africa at the games on Saturday.

In men’s hockey South Africa found themselves up against it early on after giving up a penalty flick that gave world No 1 Netherlands the lead. While they would level the scores with a spectacular first goal of the tournament. However with player’s short, South Africa struggled to contain the Dutch who raced out to 4-1 lead. While the green and gold fought back valiantly they couldn’t do enough to avoid a 5-3 opening match defeat.

Judoka Geronay Whitebooi made it through the round of 32 with an emphatic victory over Jacqueline Solis of Argentina but she would come unstuck in the Round of 16 against Natsumi Tsunoda of Japan.

Paige Badenhorst finished third in her heat in 7:39.19 to qualify for the quarter-finals of the Women’s single scull rowing event.

Meanwhile in the poolErin Gallagherfinished sixth in her heat in 57.80 to qualify 13th fastest for the evening semi-finals in the 100 metre butterfly. China’s Yufei Zhang was quickest in 56.50sec.

HOCKEY

Men’s Pool A: RESULTTeam SA 3 Netherlands 5

JUDO

Women’s -48kg elimination, round of 32: WIN!Geronay Whitebooi, ranked No39 in the world, beat her opponent Jacqueline Solis of Argentina 10-1 (ippon). The end came at 3:57 into the contest

Women’s -48kg elimination, round of 16: RESULTGeronay Whitebooi lost to Natsumi Tsunoda (Japan)

ROWING

Women’s single scull, heats: THROUGH!Paige Badenhorst finished third in her heat in 7:39.19 to qualify for the quarter-finals

SWIMMING

Women’s 100m butterfly, heats: THROUGH!Erin Gallagher finished sixth in her heat in 57.80 to qualify 13th fastest for the evening semi-finals. China’s Yufei Zhang was quickest in 56.50sec.

BADMINTON

Women’s singles, Group H (2.50pm):Johanita Scholtz vs Kim-Ga Eun (Kor)Note:Scholtz is ranked No43 in the world and Eun is ranked No13

EQUESTRIAN

Eventing, Individual dressage (In progress):Alexander Peternell

RUGBY SEVENS

Men’s semi-final (3.30pm):Team SA vs FranceTheMen’s bronze (7pm):TBCMen’s final (7.45pm):TBC

SURFING

Men’s first round, heats (from 7pm):Jordy Smith and Matt McGillivrayWomen’s first round, heats (from 11.48pm):Sarah Baum

SWIMMINGWomen’s 100m butterfly, semi-finals (8.30pm):Erin Gallagher

SKATEBOARDING

(Postponed until Monday due to weather)

Men’s street prelims:Brandon ValjaloMen’s street final:Brandon Valjalo TBC
